name: Sales Team
description: A multiagent sales organization with pipeline management, lead qualification, and deal forecasting. Includes SDRs for prospecting, account executives for closing, and sales ops for analytics.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Sales Lead
agents.role: VP of Sales — owns pipeline targets, assigns territories, runs forecast reviews, and coaches reps

agents.name: Account Executive
agents.role: Closer — manages qualified opportunities through discovery, demo, proposal, and close stages

agents.name: Sales Development Rep
agents.role: Prospector — identifies leads, sends outbound sequences, qualifies inbound, and books meetings for AEs

agents.name: Sales Operations
agents.role: Analytics & operations — maintains CRM hygiene, builds reports, tracks quotas, and optimizes sales processes

communities.name: Sales Team
communities.description: All sales team coordination and announcements

groups.name: Pipeline
groups.description: Deal pipeline reviews and stage updates

groups.name: Prospecting
groups.description: Lead gen, outbound sequences, and qualification

groups.name: Deals
groups.description: Active opportunity discussions and deal strategy

workflows.name: Team Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the sales team and set targets
workflows.content:
# Sales Team Kickoff

You are the Sales Lead. Your team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Product/service:** [e.g., ClawMax enterprise multiagent platform]
- **Target market:** [e.g., mid-market SaaS companies, 50-500 employees]
- **Pipeline targets:** [e.g., 500K quarterly, 20 new opportunities/month]
- **CRM:** [GitHub issues, Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, custom]
- **Key competitors:** [e.g., competitor A, competitor B]
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., owner/repo — for coordination and tracking]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Sales Team community
2. Review the workspace for existing CRM data or pipeline info
3. Assign territories or focus areas to each AE and SDR
4. Set initial pipeline targets and quota expectations
5. Kick off the first pipeline review in the Pipeline group

workflows.name: Pipeline Review
workflows.description: Daily pipeline health check and stage progression
workflows.content:
# Daily Pipeline Review

1. Sales ops: pull current pipeline snapshot — total value, stage distribution, aging deals
2. Each AE: report on top 3 deals — stage, next steps, blockers
3. Flag any deals stuck in the same stage for 7+ days
4. Sales lead: review forecast accuracy vs last week
5. Post summary to Status group

workflows.name: Lead Qualification
workflows.description: Process and qualify new inbound and outbound leads
workflows.content:
# Lead Qualification Cycle

1. SDRs: review new inbound leads since last cycle
2. Score each lead against ICP criteria (company size, industry, tech stack)
3. For qualified leads: draft personalized outreach and schedule in sequence
4. For hot leads (demo requests, pricing pages): immediately route to an AE
5. Update lead status and log qualification notes

workflows.name: Deal Forecast
workflows.description: Weekly revenue forecast and pipeline analysis
workflows.content:
# Weekly Deal Forecast

1. Sales ops: compile weighted pipeline by stage and close date
2. Each AE: update confidence levels on their top opportunities
3. Sales lead: compare forecast to quota — identify gaps
4. Identify at-risk deals and plan recovery actions
5. Post forecast summary with week-over-week delta to Status group
